title: Mitrephora ovata
intro: Mitrephora ovata is a plant species in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as a medicinal plant. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mitrephora ovata include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to exhibit potent cytotoxic and anti-cancer activities. In traditional medicine, Mitrephora ovata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being processed into capsules or tablets for modern herbal supplements.
taxonomy: Mitrephora ovata belongs to the family Annonaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Mitrephora. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. Further classification would place it in the order Magnoliales. The species is also known to be closely related to other flowering plants in the same family.
